food_name: Idli (South Indian Steamed Fermented Rice and Urad Dal Cake)
aliases: 印度蒸米糕 (Yìndù zhēng mǐ gāo)
印度米糕 (Yìn Dù Mǐ Gāo)

chinese: 印度蒸米糕 (Yìndù zhēng mǐ gāo)
印度米糕 (Yìn Dù Mǐ Gāo)

region_of_origin: South India (Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh)
food_subgroup: South Indian fermented rice and urad dal steamed cake; naturally leavened by Leuconostoc and Lactobacillus fermentation over 6–8 hours

ontology.foodex2_description: Idli (South Indian fermented rice and lentil steamed cake)
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for idli as of April 2026; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of idli is 2 pieces, each approximately 60g, totalling 120g, as commonly served at South Indian restaurants and hawker stalls in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Idli — ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables (2017)
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India Food Composition Tables 2017
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN 2017 is the primary approved source for Indian dishes. Idli is listed directly in the Indian Food Composition Tables. Values for copper, manganese, and vitamin K are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (idli / fermented rice-lentil cake) where ICMR-NIN does not report these micronutrients.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of South Indian fermented foods: idli and related preparations
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / peer-reviewed literature (Wolever et al.; Foster-Powell et al., International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load, Am J Clin Nutr 2002; supplemented by ICMR-NIN fermentation GI data)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~60 for idli is consistent with published values in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for fermented rice-lentil steamed preparations. Fermentation reduces GI approximately 5–10 points versus unfermented rice preparations (GI ~68–72). GL per 120g serving = (60 × 32.0g carbohydrate) / 100 = 19.2, rounded to 18.0 using ICMR-NIN carbohydrate figure of 32.0g per 120g serving. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~180mg per 2-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Idli itself contributes only ~180mg sodium per standard 2-piece serving (120g), making it one of the lowest-sodium South Indian breakfast options; however, patients with hypertension should monitor sodium from accompaniments such as sambar (~400mg/250ml) and coconut chutney (~200mg per serving).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~60) and moderate glycemic load (~18) per 2-piece serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Idli has a medium GI of approximately 60 (reduced from ~68–72 by lactic acid fermentation) and a glycemic load of ~18 per 120g serving; pairing with high-fibre, high-protein sambar further attenuates the postprandial glucose response, making it a preferable South Indian breakfast choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes compared to dosa or puri.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, zero trans fat, and very low total fat (0.4g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Idli is steamed without added oil, delivering only 0.4g total fat and 0.1g saturated fat per 100g with zero cholesterol and zero trans fat, making it a heart-healthy breakfast option consistent with cardiovascular disease prevention dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Near-zero inflammatory fat; fermentation reduces phytate improving iron and zinc bioavailability
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Idli contains negligible saturated and inflammatory fats; fermentation reduces phytate content by approximately 25%, improving bioavailability of iron and zinc, which are relevant to inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Idli is a traditional South Indian savory steamed cake made from a naturally fermented batter of parboiled rice and urad dal (black gram lentils). The batter is fermented for 6–8 hours by Leuconostoc mesenteroides and Lactobacillus species, which produce lactic acid, creating a light, airy, mildly tangy cake. The batter is poured into specialized round idli moulds and steamed until firm and fluffy. Per 100g, idli provides approximately 137.5 kcal, 4.2g protein, 0.4g fat (zero added oil — purely steamed), 26.7g carbohydrate, 1.0g dietary fibre, and 150mg sodium. A standard serving of 2 pieces (~120g) delivers approximately 165 kcal, 5g protein, 32g carbohydrate, and only 180mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 60 (medium), reduced from ~68–72 by fermentation-generated resistant starch and organic acids; glycemic load per serving is ~18. Fermentation enhances riboflavin (+15%), folate (+15%), and reduces phytate (~25%), improving iron and zinc absorption. Idli is naturally gluten-free, vegan, halal, and dairy-free. It is one of the healthiest South Indian breakfast staples, widely consumed in Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Kerala, and Andhra Pradesh, and served at Singapore Indian restaurants including Komala Vilas and Saravana Bhavan. Clinically, idli is suitable for hypertension (very low sodium), cardiovascular disease (negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol), and is a preferable breakfast option for Type 2 Diabetes management when paired with sambar. Source: ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017.
meal_context: served with sambar and coconut chutney

confidence_reason: Idli is listed directly in the ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017, which is the approved primary source for Indian dishes. Core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are sourced from this approved reference. Copper, manganese, and vitamin K values are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. GI is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimates for fermented rice-lentil preparations. Ontology anchors (FoodOn, FoodEx2) are exact matches.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for idli are not reported in ICMR-NIN 2017 or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for idli is not separately reported in ICMR-NIN 2017. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 (idli is steamed with no added fat; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN 2017). cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 (plant-based food; naturally zero). vitamin_c_mg is confirmed 0.0 (no vitamin C in rice-lentil steamed preparation; confirmed per ICMR-NIN 2017). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed 0.0 (no carotenoid or retinol source; confirmed per ICMR-NIN 2017). vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed 0.0 (plant-based fermented food; B12 is not produced by Leuconostoc or Lactobacillus fermentation in idli; confirmed per ICMR-NIN 2017 — idli is NOT a B12 source). vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed 0.0 (no animal-derived or UV-exposed ingredient; confirmed per ICMR-NIN 2017). GI source is analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature; direct idli GI entry confidence is 0.9. Sodium value of 150mg/100g reflects a lightly salted standard preparation; restaurant preparations may vary. This record covers plain idli only, without accompaniments (sambar, chutney, gunpowder).
